---
title: "Proyecto"
subtitle: "Elementos centrales de nuestra investigación"
title-block-banner: "#212626"
title-block-banner-color: body
toc: false
format: html
freeze: true
---

<style>
  d-title {
    display: none;
  }
</style>

:::{.column-page layout="[1,1]" style="float:left;"}

<a href="https://galah.ala.org.au/">
![](/images/hex/market-justice.png){style="width:73%;"}
</a>

# Justicia de mercado

La justicia de mercado es un concepto planteado por [Robert E. Lane (1986)](https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/american-political-science-review/article/abs/market-justice-political-justice/B6D76C2B6DBE20FE462CFFEC75983C3E), que se comprende como la idea en que las personas consideran justo que el acceso y la calidad de los servicios estén determinados por la capacidad de pago individual.<br> <br>
Descripción del concepto y cómo se vincula con el proyecto...

:::

***

:::{.column-page layout="[1,1]" style="float:left;"}



<a href="https://galaxias.ala.org.au/">
![](/images/hex/deserve.jpg){style="width:73%;"}
</a>



# Merecimiento

[{galaxias}](https://galaxias.ala.org.au/) is an R and Python interface designed to simplify the process of converting biodiversity data into Darwin Core Archives (DwCA), facilitating data submission to infrastructures such as the Atlas of Living Australia (ALA) or the Global Biodiversity Information Facility (GBIF).<br> <br>
Visit the **[{galaxias} website](http://galaxias.ala.org.au/)** to learn more.<br><br>
If you have any questions, comments, or spot any bugs, [email us](mailto:support@ala.org.au) or report an issue on the [R package GitHub page](https://github.com/AtlasOfLivingAustralia/galaxias) or the [Python package Github page](https://github.com/AtlasOfLivingAustralia/galaxias-python)

:::

***

:::{.column-page layout="[1,1]" style="float:left;"}



<a href="https://corella.ala.org.au/">
![](/images/hex/comparative.jpg){style="width:73%;"}
</a>



# Estudios comparativos

[{corella}](https://corella.ala.org.au/) is an R and Python package that helps users standardize their data using the [Darwin Core](https://dwc.tdwg.org/) data standard, used for biodiversity data like species occurrences. corella provides tools to prepare, manipulate and validate data against the standard’s criteria. Once standardized, data can be subsequently shared as a [Darwin Core Archive](https://ipt.gbif.org/manual/en/ipt/latest/dwca-guide#what-is-darwin-core-archive-dwc-a) and published to open data infrastructures like the [Atlas of Living Australia](https://www.ala.org.au/) and [GBIF](https://www.gbif.org/).<br> <br>
Visit the **[{corella} website](https://corella.ala.org.au/)** to learn more.<br><br>
If you have any questions, comments, or spot any bugs, [email us](mailto:support@ala.org.au) or report an issue on the [R package GitHub page](https://github.com/AtlasOfLivingAustralia/corella) or the [Python package Github page](https://github.com/AtlasOfLivingAustralia/corella-python)

:::

***

:::{.column-page layout="[1,1]" style="float:left;"}



<a href="https://delma.ala.org.au/">
![](/images/hex/long.jpg){style="width:73%;"}
</a>



# Análisis longitudinales

[{delma}](https://delma.ala.org.au/) is an R and Python package for converting metadata statements written in R Markdown or Quarto markdown to [Ecological Metadata Language](https://eml.ecoinformatics.org/) (EML). EML is a common framework for describing ecological datasets so they can be shared and reused. delma supports users to write metadata statements in markdown, R Markdown or Quarto markdown for greater transparency and ease-of-use, then convert them to EML for efficient transfer.<br> <br>
Visit the **[{delma} website](https://delma.ala.org.au/)** to learn more.<br><br>
If you have any questions, comments, or spot any bugs, [email us](mailto:support@ala.org.au) or report an issue on the [R package GitHub page](https://github.com/AtlasOfLivingAustralia/delma) or the [Python package Github page](https://github.com/AtlasOfLivingAustralia/delma-python)

:::
